title: My Terminal Setup in 2024
body:
I spend most of my day in the terminal, so I've put some effort into making it comfortable. Here's what I'm currently using.

I switched from iTerm2 to Ghostty a few months ago and haven't looked back. It's fast, minimal, and the configuration is straightforward.

For my shell, I'm still on zsh with a pretty minimal setup. I use starship for the prompt, which gives me git status and language versions without any performance hit.

The key tools I reach for daily: fzf for fuzzy finding, ripgrep for searching, and zoxide for directory jumping. These three have probably saved me hundreds of hours.
